name: Run-Based Progression
description: A progression structure where power, resources, map state, and build choices accumulate inside a single run and reset or convert when the run ends.
design_notes: Run-based progression depends on a strict boundary between temporary run state and persistent account state. Designers should know which currencies, unlock flags, relics, map reveals, and event outcomes are reset, converted, or archived. Technical systems should treat run events as an ordered log so summaries, replays, and bug reports can reconstruct the path.
design_purpose: Give each run a visible arc from weak start to specialized build.
Support procedural variety without requiring permanent character growth every session.
Define clean conversion points for post-run summaries, unlocks, and meta rewards.
player_fantasy: The player feels a fragile run develop into a unique build with its own history and stakes.

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Run identity shared by events, rewards, map state, and summary.

parameters.name: floor_index
parameters.description: Current progression position used by rewards, enemies, and shops.

parameters.name: run_state_snapshot
parameters.typical_range: build, resources, map, modifiers, rng cursors
parameters.description: Serializable state needed to restore or summarize the run.

parameters.name: run_event_log_id
parameters.typical_range: stable log id for ordered run events
parameters.description: Event stream used for replay, debugging, and post-run review.

parameters.name: unlock_pool_id
parameters.description: Persistent availability pool that feeds run rewards.

parameters.name: conversion_policy_id
parameters.description: Rule applied to run assets when the run ends.

parameters.name: difficulty_scaling_curve_id
parameters.description: Curve used to increase pressure as the run advances.

edge_cases: The player earns a permanent unlock while holding temporary run items with similar ids.
A save migrates after reward definitions change but before the run reaches its summary screen.
A reconnect restores account state but not the current run_state_snapshot.
A daily run must forbid some meta progression while still recording results.
A bugged run event is rolled back without corrupting account unlocks.
common_bugs: Temporary run currency is saved in the same wallet as account currency.
Unlocks are granted when a reward is previewed instead of when it is committed.
Difficulty scaling reads total account progress and unintentionally punishes experienced players in fresh runs.
Run event logs omit shop purchases or skipped rewards, making summaries misleading.
Save migration converts removed run items into permanent unlocks by matching display names.
balancing_notes: Strong run growth is satisfying when early weakness remains short and readable.
Meta progression should widen future possibilities more than it invalidates current-run skill.
Difficulty curves need to account for optional rewards and skipped rooms, not only floor_index.
accessibility_notes: Let players review current run build, modifiers, and recent decisions in a readable summary.
Avoid hiding critical progression state inside tiny icons or transient notifications.
Offer suspend-and-resume support where possible so long runs are not inaccessible to players with limited session length.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep RunState and AccountState in separate services or save files. Commit run events with run_id ordering, recompute derived build stats after each reward, and apply conversion_policy_id only once when run_end_reason is final.
implementation_notes.godot: Use an autoload run manager for current run state and a separate account profile resource for unlocks. Save floor_index, active rewards, modifiers, and RNG cursors together, then archive the run event log for summaries.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use GameInstance or subsystem ownership for run data and a separate SaveGame object for account progression. Networked runs should resolve reward commits and floor transitions on the authority before replicating snapshots.
implementation_notes.web: Store run records separately from account records in IndexedDB or server storage. Process run events through a reducer so refresh, replay, and debugging can reconstruct run_state_snapshot deterministically.

relationships.reason: Run-based progression supplies the reset boundary that makes permadeath legible and enforceable.
Run reward choices give each run build identity without requiring permanent unlocks.
Persistent account unlocks must be separated from run state to avoid erasing run-based stakes.

example_games.title: Hades
example_games.note: Uses temporary run growth alongside persistent unlock systems.

example_games.title: Dead Cells
example_games.note: Builds power during a run while some unlock progress persists outside it.
